 {"id":"brenner_bot-0xzg","title":"Task: Coverage Threshold Enforcement (80%)","description":"# Goal
Raise test coverage thresholds to 80% and enforce per-file minimum coverage.

# Current State
```typescript
// vitest.config.ts (current)
coverage: {
  thresholds: {
    lines: 50,
    functions: 50,
    branches: 50,
    statements: 50,
  }
}
```

# Target State
```typescript
// vitest.config.ts (target)
coverage: {
  provider: 'v8',
  reporter: ['text', 'lcov', 'html', 'json'],
  reportsDirectory: './coverage',
  include: ['src/**/*.ts', 'src/**/*.tsx'],
  exclude: [
    'src/**/*.test.ts',
    'src/**/*.test.tsx',
    'src/__fixtures__/**',
    'src/types/**',
    '**/*.d.ts',
  ],
  thresholds: {
    // Global thresholds
    lines: 80,
    functions: 80,
    branches: 75,
    statements: 80,
    
    // Per-file enforcement
    perFile: true,
    
    // Critical files with higher thresholds
    'src/lib/artifact-merge.ts': {
      lines: 90,
      functions: 90,
      branches: 85,
    },
    'src/lib/delta-parser.ts': {
      lines: 90,
      functions: 90,
      branches: 85,
    },
  },
  
  // Fail CI on threshold violation
  skipFull: false,
  
  // Check for uncovered lines
  checkCoverage: true,
}
```

# CI Integration
Update GitHub Actions workflow:
```yaml
# .github/workflows/test.yml
- name: Run tests with coverage
  run: bun test --coverage
  
- name: Check coverage thresholds
  run: |
    # Parse coverage report and fail if below threshold
    bun run coverage:check
    
- name: Upload coverage to Codecov
  uses: codecov/codecov-action@v3
  with:
    files: ./apps/web/coverage/lcov.info
    fail_ci_if_error: true
```

# Coverage Badge
Add to README:
```markdown
[![Coverage](https://codecov.io/gh/user/brenner_bot/branch/main/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/user/brenner_bot)
```

# Gradual Rollout Plan
1. Week 1: Set global to 60%, add perFile
2. Week 2: Raise to 70%, identify low-coverage files
3. Week 3: Raise to 80%, enforce on all files
4. Ongoing: Add per-file exceptions only with justification

# Coverage Exclusion Guidelines
Exclude from coverage (with comment justification):
- Type definitions (*.d.ts)
- Test files and fixtures
- Generated code
- Configuration files
- Development-only code

DO NOT exclude:
- Complex business logic
- Error handling paths
- Edge cases

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Global threshold at 80% lines
- [ ] Per-file enforcement enabled
- [ ] Critical files at 90%
- [ ] CI fails on threshold violation
- [ ] Coverage badge in README
- [ ] Coverage report uploaded to Codecov

 {"id":"brenner_bot-1cv2","title":"Tutorial UX: Mobile Experience & Limitations","description":"# Purpose

Document and handle the reality that tutorial steps requiring terminal commands cannot be fully completed on mobile devices. Ensure mobile users have a good experience anyway.

---

# The Reality

Many tutorial steps require:
- Running terminal commands (git, bun, ./brenner)
- Copying multi-line code blocks
- Running AI agents (Claude Code, Codex)

**These cannot be done on mobile.** A phone or tablet user physically cannot:
- Run a terminal
- Clone a repo
- Execute CLI commands

---

# Design Decisions

## Option A: Gate terminal steps to desktop (CHOSEN)

Show a clear message on terminal-required steps when on mobile:
\`\`\`
📱 This step requires a desktop computer

The commands below need to be run in a terminal on your 
Mac, Windows (WSL), or Linux computer.

You can:
• Read through this step to understand what's involved
• Bookmark this page and return on desktop
• Continue to the next step (some content is mobile-friendly)
\`\`\`

## Option B: Mobile-optimized read-only mode

Automatically switch to "read-only" mode on mobile:
- All content visible
- Code blocks show but note "run on desktop"
- Progress tracking still works
- Can complete "understanding" checkboxes, not "execution" checkboxes

---

# Implementation

## 1. Device Detection
Use user-agent or viewport width to detect mobile.

## 2. Step Metadata
Add \`requiresDesktop: boolean\` to step data:
\`\`\`typescript
interface TutorialStep {
  // ...existing fields
  requiresDesktop?: boolean;
  mobileAlternative?: string; // What to show on mobile instead
}
\`\`\`

## 3. Mobile Message Component
\`\`\`tsx
function MobileDesktopGate({ children, step }: Props) {
  const isMobile = useIsMobile();
  
  if (isMobile && step.requiresDesktop) {
    return <MobileNotice step={step} />;
  }
  
  return children;
}
\`\`\`

## 4. Which Steps Need Desktop?

**Quick Start**:
- Step 2 (Prerequisites): Desktop required
- Step 3 (Clone & Install): Desktop required
- Step 4 (Search Corpus): Desktop required
- Step 5 (Build Excerpt): Desktop required
- Step 6 (First Session): Desktop required
- Step 7 (Understand Output): Mobile OK (reading)

**Agent-Assisted**:
- Steps 2-7: Desktop required
- Step 1, 8: Mobile OK (reading/understanding)

**Multi-Agent**:
- All steps: Desktop required (infrastructure setup)

---

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Mobile detection works reliably
- [ ] Desktop-required steps show clear message on mobile
- [ ] Users can still read step content on mobile
- [ ] Progress tracking works on mobile (marks steps as "viewed")
- [ ] Desktop users see no change","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T15:34:28.987529053-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:08:48.244621638-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-1cv2","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-do3j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T15:35:25.632137409-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-1es5","title":"Spec: Experiment Result Classification and Interpretation","description":"# Goal
Define how experiment results are classified and interpreted into DELTAs.

# Two-Stage Process (Key Insight)

**Stage 1: Classification** (structured, can be assisted)
- What KIND of result is this?
- Pass/fail/error/metrics/observation/anomaly?

**Stage 2: Interpretation** (judgment, guidelines only)
- What does this mean for hypotheses?
- What DELTA should be created?

# Stage 1: Result Classification

## The Taxonomy (Restored)
```typescript
type ExperimentResultType = 
  | "pass"           // Test completed, outcome matches expectation
  | "fail"           // Test completed, outcome contradicts expectation
  | "error"          // Test couldn't complete (crash, timeout, missing deps)
  | "metrics"        // Quantitative measurements without clear pass/fail
  | "observation"    // Qualitative finding requiring interpretation
  | "anomaly";       // Unexpected result that doesn't fit any prediction

interface ClassifiedResult {
  experiment_id: string;
  test_id: string;
  
  // Classification
  result_type: ExperimentResultType;
  confidence: "high" | "medium" | "low";
  
  // Extracted data (type-specific)
  metrics?: Record<string, number>;
  observation?: string;
  error_message?: string;
  
  // Which hypotheses this relates to
  hypothesis_refs: string[];  // ["H1", "H2"]
}
```

## Classification CLI (Assistant, Not Automator)
```bash
# Suggest classification based on output
./brenner.ts experiment classify \
  --session RS-20251230-bio-rrp \
  --experiment exp-a1b2c3

# Output:
# Result type: metrics (confidence: high)
# Extracted metrics:
#   bleu_vanilla: 0.42
#   bleu_rrp: 0.48
#   bleu_freq_penalty: 0.45
# 
# Related hypotheses: H1, H2 (based on test T1 definition)
# 
# Suggested interpretation pattern: "metrics_comparison"
# See: ./brenner.ts experiment interpret --pattern metrics_comparison
```

# Stage 2: Interpretation Patterns

Classification tells you WHAT you have. Interpretation decides WHAT IT MEANS.

## Pattern: pass → Predictions Update
**When**: Test passed, result matches hypothesis prediction
**DELTA**:
```json
{
  "operation": "EDIT",
  "section": "predictions_table",
  "target_id": "P1",
  "payload": {
    "actual_H1": "Confirmed: 0.03 BLEU difference, within equivalence threshold"
  },
  "rationale": "T1 passed: equivalence holds at tested scale"
}
```

## Pattern: fail → Hypothesis Weakening
**When**: Test clearly contradicts a hypothesis
**DELTA**:
```json
{
  "operation": "EDIT",
  "section": "hypothesis_slate",
  "target_id": "H1",
  "payload": {
    "status": "weakened",
    "evidence_against": "T1 showed 15% quality gap at 16k context"
  },
  "rationale": "H1 predicted equivalence; T1 found significant divergence"
}
```

## Pattern: error → Test Blocked
**When**: Experiment couldn't complete
**DELTA**:
```json
{
  "operation": "EDIT",
  "section": "discriminative_tests",
  "target_id": "T1",
  "payload": {
    "status": "blocked",
    "blocker": "OOM at 16k context, need 32GB instance"
  },
  "rationale": "T1 could not complete due to resource constraints"
}
```

## Pattern: metrics → Attach Raw Data
**When**: Have numbers but interpretation unclear
**DELTA**:
```json
{
  "operation": "EDIT",
  "section": "discriminative_tests",
  "target_id": "T1",
  "payload": {
    "actual_results": {
      "bleu_vanilla": 0.42,
      "bleu_rrp": 0.48,
      "bleu_freq_penalty": 0.45
    },
    "status": "awaiting_interpretation"
  },
  "rationale": "T1 produced metrics. Interpretation TBD."
}
```

## Pattern: anomaly → Register for Investigation
**When**: Result doesn't match ANY prediction
**DELTA**:
```json
{
  "operation": "ADD",
  "section": "anomaly_register",
  "payload": {
    "name": "Latency spike at 4k boundary",
    "observation": "RRP showed 3x latency at exactly 4096 tokens",
    "conflicts_with": ["H1", "H2"],
    "status": "active"
  },
  "rationale": "No hypothesis predicted latency effects"
}
```

## Pattern: observation → Critique or Assumption
**When**: Qualitative insight from running experiment
**DELTA** (if it challenges framing):
```json
{
  "operation": "ADD",
  "section": "adversarial_critique",
  "payload": {
    "name": "Metric choice confound",
    "attack": "BLEU may not capture the coherence benefits RRP provides",
    "evidence": "Running T1 revealed outputs differed in structure, not token overlap",
    "current_status": "Observed during experiment, needs investigation"
  },
  "rationale": "T1 execution revealed potential metric confound"
}
```

# When Patterns Don't Fit
- If result is ambiguous between patterns → discuss in thread
- If multiple patterns apply → create multiple DELTAs
- If no pattern fits → add to anomaly_register with status: "unclassified"

# CLI Helper
```bash
# Show interpretation guidance for a pattern
./brenner.ts experiment interpret --pattern anomaly

# Interactive mode: walk through classification and interpretation
./brenner.ts experiment interpret --interactive \
  --session RS-20251230-bio-rrp \
  --experiment exp-a1b2c3
```

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ExperimentResultType taxonomy defined
- [ ] ClassifiedResult schema defined
- [ ] experiment classify command implemented
- [ ] All interpretation patterns documented in specs/
- [ ] experiment interpret helper command
- [ ] Guidance on when to ask for help

# Relationship to Other Work
- Depends on: Execution protocol (brenner_bot-t62l)
- Uses: Existing delta-parser and artifact-merge
- Enables: Artifact evolution from experiment evidence","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-30T18:51:12.453215707-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T00:21:04.888114637-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T00:21:04.888114637-05:00","close_reason":"All 4 child tasks completed: ExperimentResult schema (1es5.1), artifact types (1es5.2), experiment encode CLI (1es5.3), golden fixtures + tests (1es5.4)","labels":["infrastructure","protocol","research-loop"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-1es5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-t62l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T18:52:25.940633427-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-1es5","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.12","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T18:59:54.950352935-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"brenner_bot-43o6","title":"TASK: Define Brenner Loop Session Phases","description":"
## What ARE the Phases?

We have a "Phase Timeline Component" (reew.2) but no definition of what phases actually exist in a Brenner Loop session.

### The Brenner Loop Phases

Based on Sydney Brenner's methodology, a session should flow through these phases:

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                       BRENNER LOOP SESSION PHASES                       │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                         │
│  1. INTAKE          2. REFINEMENT       3. TESTING          4. SYNTHESIS │
│  ───────────        ─────────────       ───────────         ─────────── │
│  │                  │                   │                   │           │
│  ▼                  ▼                   ▼                   ▼           │
│  ┌────────┐         ┌────────┐          ┌────────┐         ┌────────┐  │
│  │ State  │  ────▶  │ Refine │  ────▶   │  Test  │ ────▶   │ Brief  │  │
│  │ Hypo   │         │ + Lock │          │ Queue  │         │ Output │  │
│  └────────┘         └────────┘          └────────┘         └────────┘  │
│                                                                         │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Phase 1: INTAKE (Hypothesis Formation)
**Goal**: Capture the initial hypothesis in structured form

Activities:
- User states their hypothesis
- Socratic intake form guides refinement
- Core fields populated (statement, mechanism, predictions)
- Optional: Devil's Advocate early challenge

Transitions to Refinement when:
- Core hypothesis fields are complete
- User clicks "Proceed to Refinement"

### Phase 2: REFINEMENT (Operator Application)
**Goal**: Apply Brenner operators to sharpen the hypothesis

Activities:
- Level Split (Σ): Identify levels of explanation
- Exclusion Test (⊘): Design discriminative tests
- Object Transpose (⟳): Change reference frames
- Scale Check (⊙): Check scale-dependence

Key Moments:
- Agent consultation (Devil's Advocate, Experiment Designer)
- Pre-registration of predictions
- Prediction LOCKING before evidence

Transitions to Testing when:
- At least one prediction is locked
- User has a test queue
- User clicks "Begin Testing"

### Phase 3: TESTING (Evidence Collection)
**Goal**: Execute tests and record evidence

Activities:
- Work through test queue
- Record actual observations
- Update confidence based on outcomes
- What-if analysis for remaining tests

Key Moments:
- Confidence updates (Bayesian-style)
- Hypothesis evolution (if needed)
- Potential falsification

Transitions to Synthesis when:
- Test queue exhausted OR
- Hypothesis falsified OR
- User has sufficient confidence

### Phase 4: SYNTHESIS (Artifact Generation)
**Goal**: Produce durable research artifact

Activities:
- Generate Research Brief
- Summarize evidence trajectory
- Document lessons learned
- Export in desired format

Key Moments:
- Agent summary (Brenner Channeler)
- Citation compilation
- Export to Markdown/PDF

Session closes when:
- Research Brief generated
- User marks session complete

### Phase Data Model
```typescript
type SessionPhase = 'intake' | 'refinement' | 'testing' | 'synthesis';

interface PhaseProgress {
  phase: SessionPhase;
  startedAt: Date;
  completedAt?: Date;
  
  // Phase-specific progress
  intake?: {
    coreFieldsComplete: boolean;
    predictionsLocked: number;
  };
  refinement?: {
    operatorsApplied: OperatorType[];
    agentConsultations: number;
  };
  testing?: {
    testsPlanned: number;
    testsCompleted: number;
    confidenceHistory: number[];
  };
  synthesis?: {
    briefGenerated: boolean;
    exported: boolean;
  };
}
```

### File Location
`apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/phases.ts` - Phase definitions
`apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/transitions.ts` - Transition logic

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All four phases clearly defined
- [ ] Transition conditions specified
- [ ] Phase progress tracked in session state
- [ ] Timeline component can render phases

### Depends On
- 1v26.1 (Session Data Model - needs PhaseProgress)

Build a **world-class tutorial system** for brennerbot.org that onboards researchers to actually USE the Brenner method for their own scientific questions — not just read about it.

This is the bridge from "interesting concept" to "I'm using this for my research."

---

# Why This Matters (Strategic Rationale)

## The Adoption Gap
BrennerBot currently excels at **exposition** (distillations, corpus, method docs) but lacks **activation** (getting users to apply it). Without a tutorial system:
- Curious researchers read, nod, and leave
- Power users who could benefit never discover the CLI workflows
- The multi-agent cockpit remains hidden complexity

## The Opportunity
A great tutorial system transforms BrennerBot from "educational resource" to "research tool I actually use." It compounds:
- Each successful user becomes a potential contributor
- Real usage surfaces bugs and UX issues
- Research artifacts become case studies for future users

## Why Now
- The core system is stable (corpus, distillations, CLI, Agent Mail integration all work)
- The method documentation is comprehensive (operators, schemas, specs)
- We have three natural learning paths that match different user sophistication levels
- The ACFS project provides a proven tutorial architecture to adapt

---

# Design Philosophy

## Three Learning Paths (Progressive Complexity)

### Path A: Quick Start (~30 min)
**Audience**: Curious researchers, first-timers
**Promise**: "Apply Brenner's method to your own research question in 30 minutes"
**Key Constraint**: No complex infrastructure (no Agent Mail, no ntm)
**Output**: A research artifact (hypotheses, tests, assumptions) for their own problem

### Path B: Agent-Assisted Research (~45 min)
**Audience**: Power users with Claude Code or Codex
**Promise**: "Have your AI agent study the repo and run the Brenner process for you"
**Key Insight**: The AI agent internalizes the methodology, not just executes commands
**Output**: Agent-produced research artifacts with human oversight

### Path C: Multi-Agent Cockpit (~2 hours)
**Audience**: Advanced practitioners
**Promise**: "Orchestrate Claude + GPT + Gemini as a coordinated research group"
**Key Infrastructure**: ntm, Agent Mail, role-specific prompts
**Output**: Triangulated research artifacts with delta merging and linting

 {"id":"brenner_bot-4buj","title":"Task: Define Research Program Schema","description":"# Task: Define Research Program Schema

## Objective
Define TypeScript types and Zod schemas for research programs that group related sessions.

## Implementation Details

### File Location
`apps/web/src/lib/research-program.ts`

### Core Schemas
```typescript
import { z } from 'zod';

export const ProgramStatusSchema = z.enum([
  'active',     // Ongoing research
  'paused',     // Temporarily suspended
  'completed',  // Research question answered
  'abandoned',  // Stopped without conclusion
]);

export const ResearchProgramSchema = z.object({
  id: z.string().regex(/^RP-[A-Z0-9]+-[a-z-]+$/),  // e.g., RP-20251230-cell-fate
  name: z.string().min(5).max(100),
  description: z.string().min(20),
  researchQuestion: z.string().min(10),
  sessions: z.array(z.string()),  // Session IDs
  status: ProgramStatusSchema,
  createdAt: z.string().datetime(),
  updatedAt: z.string().datetime(),
  completedAt: z.string().datetime().optional(),
  tags: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
  notes: z.string().optional(),
  metadata: z.record(z.unknown()).optional(),
});

export type ResearchProgram = z.infer<typeof ResearchProgramSchema>;
export type ProgramStatus = z.infer<typeof ProgramStatusSchema>;
```

### ID Generation
```typescript
export function generateProgramId(date: Date, slug: string): string {
  const dateStr = date.toISOString().slice(0, 10).replace(/-/g, '');
  const normalizedSlug = slug.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9-]/g, '-').slice(0, 30);
  return `RP-${dateStr}-${normalizedSlug}`;
}
```

### Program Metrics Type
```typescript
export interface ProgramMetrics {
  programId: string;
  
  // Hypothesis metrics
  totalHypotheses: number;
  activeHypotheses: number;
  killedHypotheses: number;
  validatedHypotheses: number;
  dormantHypotheses: number;
  killRate: number;  // killed / (killed + validated + active)
  
  // Test metrics
  totalTests: number;
  completedTests: number;
  blockedTests: number;
  testExecutionRate: number;
  
  // Session metrics
  sessionCount: number;
  averageSessionScore: number;
  scoreTrend: 'improving' | 'declining' | 'stable';
  
  // Time metrics
  daysActive: number;
  lastActivityAt: string;
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Program schema with all required fields
- [ ] ID generation follows pattern
- [ ] Metrics type captures key indicators
- [ ] Validation for program creation
- [ ] Status transitions defined

## Dependencies
- Feature: Hypothesis Registry Schema (for hypothesis metrics)
- Feature: Brenner Method Scorecard (for score metrics)

 {"id":"brenner_bot-x0o2","title":"Task: Define Test Record and Execution Schemas","description":"# Task: Define Test Record and Execution Schemas

## Objective
Create TypeScript types and Zod schemas for test records, predictions, and execution tracking.

## Implementation Details

### File Location
`apps/web/src/lib/test-binding.ts`

### Core Schemas
```typescript
import { z } from 'zod';

export const TestStatusSchema = z.enum([
  'designed',     // Test designed but not yet started
  'in_progress',  // Test is being executed
  'completed',    // Test has results
  'blocked',      // Test cannot proceed (resources, prerequisites)
]);

export const PredictionSchema = z.object({
  hypothesisId: z.string(),
  prediction: z.string().min(5),  // What this hypothesis predicts
  rationale: z.string().optional(),  // Why this prediction follows
});

export const TestRecordSchema = z.object({
  id: z.string().regex(/^T-[A-Z0-9]+-\d{3}$/),
  description: z.string().min(10),
  targetHypotheses: z.array(PredictionSchema).min(1),
  designedInSession: z.string(),
  designedBy: z.string().optional(),
  status: TestStatusSchema,
  methodology: z.string().optional(),  // How to execute the test
  resources: z.string().optional(),  // What's needed
  execution: z.lazy(() => TestExecutionSchema).optional(),
  createdAt: z.string().datetime(),
  updatedAt: z.string().datetime(),
});

export const ConfidenceSchema = z.enum(['high', 'medium', 'low']);

export const TestExecutionSchema = z.object({
  executedAt: z.string().datetime(),
  executedBy: z.string().optional(),
  result: z.string().min(5),  // The raw observation/finding
  matchedPredictions: z.array(z.string()),  // hypothesis IDs
  violatedPredictions: z.array(z.string()),  // hypothesis IDs
  confidence: ConfidenceSchema,
  notes: z.string().optional(),
  artifacts: z.array(z.string()).optional(),  // Links to data, figures, etc.
});

export type TestRecord = z.infer<typeof TestRecordSchema>;
export type TestExecution = z.infer<typeof TestExecutionSchema>;
export type Prediction = z.infer<typeof PredictionSchema>;
export type TestStatus = z.infer<typeof TestStatusSchema>;
export type Confidence = z.infer<typeof ConfidenceSchema>;
```

### ID Generation
```typescript
export function generateTestId(sessionId: string, sequence: number): string {
  const paddedSeq = sequence.toString().padStart(3, '0');
  return `T-${sessionId}-${paddedSeq}`;
}
```

### Validation
```typescript
export function validateTestRecord(data: unknown): ValidationResult {
  const result = TestRecordSchema.safeParse(data);
  if (result.success) {
    return { valid: true, errors: [] };
  }
  return {
    valid: false,
    errors: result.error.errors.map(e => `${e.path.join('.')}: ${e.message}`),
  };
}

export function validatePredictionConsistency(test: TestRecord): ValidationResult {
  const errors: string[] = [];
  
  // Check that all target hypotheses exist
  // Check that predictions are mutually distinguishable
  // Check that at least 2 hypotheses are targeted (discriminative test)
  
  if (test.targetHypotheses.length < 2) {
    errors.push('Discriminative test should target at least 2 hypotheses');
  }
  
  return { valid: errors.length === 0, errors };
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All test-related types defined with Zod
- [ ] ID generation follows pattern
- [ ] Prediction schema links to hypotheses
- [ ] Execution schema captures results and interpretation
- [ ] Validation ensures discriminative test design

## Dependencies
- Feature: Hypothesis Registry Schema (for hypothesis ID format)
